title: phyteuma orbiculare subsp. ellipticifolium
intro: Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. ellipticifolium, also known as the round-leaved rampion or rampion bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and improve liver function, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as indigestion and jaundice. The health benefits of Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. ellipticifolium include its use as a diuretic and its ability to lower blood pressure, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as hypertension and edema. Herbal preparations of Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. ellipticifolium typically involve infusions or decoctions of the roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
